title: Nursery Rhyme. CCCCLXIII. Love And Matrimony.
content:
[The practice of sowing hempseed on Allhallows Even is often alluded to by earlier writers, and Gay, in his 'Pastorals,' quotes part of the following lines as used on that occasion.]

        Hemp-seed I set,
            Hemp-seed I sow,
        The young man that I love,
            Come after me and mow!
meaning:
Unknown's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Nursery Rhyme. CCCCLXIII. Love And Matrimony."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
